preview_text:
The model rates Canada as the marginal favorite with a 41% win probability, placing them 13 percentage points ahead of Switzerland at 28%. This represents a competitive fixture where neither side commands clear dominance, though the model's assessment tilts toward the North American team.

The draw sits at 31%, suggesting meaningful daylight between Canada's win probability and a stalemate outcome. The spread across the three outcomes is relatively balanced rather than sharply concentrated, indicating the model expects a genuinely contested match where multiple results remain plausible throughout regular time.

The model and market diverge substantially here. Sportsbooks collectively favour Switzerland at 40%, a gap of 12 percentage points above the model's assessment, while placing Canada at just 30%—an 11-point downward shift from the model. Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects true match probability; the disagreement is marked enough that bettors should note the conflict before acting.
